resources.title: Introduction to JavaScript
resources.content: JavaScript is a programming language that conforms to the ECMAScript specification. It is high-level, often just-in-time compiled, and multi-paradigm. It has curly-bracket syntax, dynamic typing, prototype-based object-orientation, and first-class functions.

resources.title: Variables and Data Types
resources.content: Variables in JavaScript can be declared using var, let, or const. Data types include primitives like string, number, boolean, null, undefined, and symbol, as well as objects and functions.

resources.title: Functions and Scope
resources.content: Functions are first-class citizens in JavaScript. They can be defined using function declarations, expressions, or arrow functions. Scope determines the accessibility of variables.

resources.title: Objects and Arrays
resources.content: Objects are collections of key-value pairs. Arrays are special objects with numeric indices. Both are fundamental data structures in JavaScript.

resources.title: Asynchronous Programming
resources.content: JavaScript handles asynchronous operations using callbacks, promises, and async/await. This is crucial for non-blocking I/O operations.

resources.title: Page 1: Arrays and Linked Lists
resources.content: Learn about arrays and linked lists, including storage structure, indexing, and traversal. Practice writing code to insert, delete, and access elements.

resources.title: Page 2: Stacks and Queues
resources.content: Study stack and queue behavior, common operations, and real-world use cases. Implement both using arrays or linked lists.

resources.title: Page 3: Trees and Graphs
resources.content: Understand binary trees, traversal algorithms, and the basics of graph representations like adjacency lists and matrices.

resources.title: Page 4: Sorting Algorithms
resources.content: Review common sorting algorithms such as bubble sort, merge sort, and quick sort, and learn how to compare their performance.

resources.title: Page 5: Dynamic Programming
resources.content: Work through dynamic programming patterns like memoization and tabulation for solving optimization problems.

resources.title: Page 1: React Components
resources.content: Learn how to build components using JSX, props, and composition.

resources.title: Page 2: State and Events
resources.content: Manage state in functional components and respond to user interactions.

resources.title: Page 3: Effects and Lifecycle
resources.content: Use useEffect and understand when component updates happen.

resources.title: Page 4: Routing and Navigation
resources.content: Build multi-page experiences using React routing and navigation patterns.

resources.title: Page 5: Project Practice
resources.content: Assemble a small React app using the concepts learned so far.

resources.title: Page 1: JavaScript Basics
resources.content: Learn JavaScript syntax, variables, and basic control flow to build your first programs.

resources.title: Page 2: Functions and Scope
resources.content: Explore function declarations, arrow functions, and how JavaScript scope works.

resources.title: Page 3: Objects and Arrays
resources.content: Understand objects, arrays, and how to manipulate collections of data.

resources.title: Page 4: Asynchronous JavaScript
resources.content: Work with callbacks, promises, and async/await for non-blocking code.

resources.title: Page 5: Practical Exercises
resources.content: Practice your new skills with short exercises and code examples.
